meta.notes:
BUILD-SPEC for V2 UI reconstruction — extracted from Q23111 quote PDF + 2 drawings (22/04/2026).

DISCREPANCY: Quote PDF 'For:' field says 'ALL ABOUT KITCHENS'; drawings say 'ALL ABOUT KITCHENS / CLIENT - LAW'. 'Hudson Kitchen Solutions' appears only in the corpus folder name. customerName above honours Sean's spec; PDF authority is ALL ABOUT KITCHENS.

MATERIAL (both kitchens): Stone Ambassador Zenith Premium Range, 40mm.
EDGE PROFILE: All exposed/visible edges Mitred (the 40mm 'Angled Benchtops' style). Wall-facing edges unfinished.
INSTALL: Local install (Sunshine Coast — Buderim).

=== KITCHEN 1: DOWN STAIRS — 4 pieces, NCS line item $6,371.95 ===

Piece D1 — Corner/sink piece: 795 x 620 x 40mm
  - Joined to piece D2 by an angled mitre (drawing shows ~135deg obtuse corner, not 90deg)
  - 1x Undermount Sink cutout
  - One long edge is WALL (unfinished); other edges visible (mitred)

Piece D2 — Cooktop run: 2476 x 600 x 40mm
  - Joined to piece D1 by the same angled mitre
  - 1x Cooktop cutout
  - One long edge is WALL; other edges visible (mitred)

Piece D3 — Island bench: 1800 x 900 x 40mm
  - Standalone rectangle, no cutouts
  - All four edges visible (mitred drop on all faces) — AMBIGUOUS, drawing shows hatch marks on 3 of 4 edges; needs Jay to confirm if the 4th edge is also mitred or against a cabinet

Piece D4 — Entry benchtop: 2900 x 600 x 40mm
  - Standalone rectangular bench, no cutouts
  - One long edge labelled WALL (unfinished)
  - Other long edge + both short edges visible (mitred)
  - 'ENTRY' callout on drawing

Down Stairs totals: 4 pieces, 2 cutouts (1 sink + 1 cooktop), 1 angled mitre join.

=== KITCHEN 2: UP STAIRS — 7 pieces, NCS line item $8,968.08 ===

Main run is a U-shape with angled (non-90deg) corners around a window bay; separate small bench has a waterfall end. Quote dims are authoritative for pricing; drawing labels in red ink ('1965 INTO WINDOW', '750 INTO WINDOW') are window OPENING sizes, not piece cut sizes.

Piece U1 — 1000 x 600 x 40mm — AMBIGUOUS — likely cooktop-run end-piece; needs Jay to confirm orientation
Piece U2 — 855 x 800 x 40mm — AMBIGUOUS — likely angled return at cooktop bay (drawing shows '855' near cooktop area)
Piece U3 — 3020 x 700 x 40mm — 'Into Window — Possible Join Due to Access' (NCS flagged: install-only join may be needed onsite due to access constraints; price assumes single piece)
Piece U4 — 790 x 680 x 40mm — Into Window
Piece U5 — 1840 x 680 x 40mm — Into Window
Piece U6 — 800 x 680 x 40mm — Into Window
Piece U7 — 1840 x 700 x 40mm

Up Stairs features:
  - 1x Waterfall End (drawing shows 'WATERFALL' label on the small standalone bench, ~700-deep section)
  - 1x Undermount Sink cutout (drawing shows 'SINK U/M' on the U-shape main run)
  - 1x Cooktop cutout (drawing shows 'COOKTOP 600' — 600mm cutout — at the angled cooktop bay)

Up Stairs joins: multiple angled mitre joins between U1-U2-U3-U4-U5-U6 around the window bay. Specific edge-by-edge join assignment AMBIGUOUS — drawing has hatch marks at 4-5 join positions but exact piece-to-piece pairing needs Jay.

Up Stairs edge-by-side assignment: AMBIGUOUS for all 7 pieces — quote bundles edge pricing into the line item; drawing hatching is too dense to read per-side without Jay.

Up Stairs totals: 7 pieces, 2 cutouts (1 sink + 1 cooktop), 1 waterfall end, 4-5 angled mitre joins (AMBIGUOUS).

=== JOB-WIDE TOTALS (for cross-check during V2 build) ===
Pieces: 11
Cutouts: 4 (2 sink, 2 cooktop)
Waterfall ends: 1
Angled mitre joins: 5-6 (AMBIGUOUS — confirm with Jay)
Total area (rough, sum of length*width): Down ~5.69 m2 (0.49+1.49+1.62+1.74), Up ~9.09 m2 (0.60+0.68+2.11+0.54+1.25+0.54+1.29). Sum ~14.78 m2 across both kitchens.

=== V2 GAPS FLAGGED ===
Angled (non-90deg) mitre joins: NCS line items describe '40mm Angled Benchtops' but do not price joins separately. V2's PiecePricingBreakdown.cornerJoin currently models 90deg corner joins only. Down Stairs has 1 obtuse-angle join; Up Stairs has 4-5. Build approach for V2 reconstruction is AMBIGUOUS — confirm with Jay before Gate 3 (options: approximate as 90deg cornerJoin, model as oversize.joinCount, or leave unmodelled and absorb the gap in delta).

Quote PDF surfaces only 2 room-level bundle totals ($6,371.95 + $8,968.08); it does NOT separate materials, edges, cutouts, services. The optional materials/edges/cutouts/services blocks on `expected` therefore cannot be populated — only headline subtotal+gst+total are testable. Per-line-item delta validation is impossible against this PDF.
